comments.text:
A few days ago, I looked at how the international border through The Great Lakes was determined.
But what about the border west of Lake Superior?
It took decades to determine and wasn't just "drawn with a ruler by some guy".
Let's learn more! 🧵1/13
www.chicagotribune.com/2025/09/25/i... Now we're paying boarder control to sight see. Lake Michigan has no international boarders.
Fascinating title for the article… as though Lake Superior isn’t bisected by an international border. 😏
They do patrol international lakes like this, Great Lakes, etc but crossing the border is a gross violation of sovereignty.

It also established the international border between Lake Superior and Lake of the Woods, and reaffirmed the location of the border at the 49th parallel west of Lake Superior to the far western Rocky Mountains.
But it wasn't just border agreements. 🧵 4/8

There's an international border on Lake Champlain, just like in the Great Lakes, so the USCG patrols it. I imagine if there are state or national parks in the area they'd also have some kind of presence.
these maps are CBP propaganda because they include Chicago as if Lake Michigan were subject to the International Law of the Sea

The University of Oregon is conducting Freedom of Navigation exercises on Lake Michigan and maintain that the University of Oregon Ship Waddles was outside of Northwestern territorial waters per international treaty.
They consider the Great Lakes shorelines to be an “international border” because it is an “international waterway” which is ridiculous but was ruled legal.

There's a loophole for CBP. They're allowed enforcement for our borders up to 100 miles inland. Under the law, the Great Lakes are considered international boundaries. Even though Chicago is well inland from Canada, it's that Lake Michigan joining Lake Huron that allows its reach.

A (not so) fun reminder that the entirety of Michigan is within Border Patrol jurisdiction or whatever.
Great Lakes count as international borders.
(ugh sorry for deletes, should not lawpost while multitasking) The statute does not say international boundary and the reg defines terms in a way that excludes (our part of) Lake Michigan

I’ve seen this graphic before which seems to imply there’s an international border on Lake Michigan and…there isn’t? Maybe the law is written to designate the shores of Lake Michigan as an international border for some reason, I haven’t looked it up. But it’s weird.
They claim authority over pretty much the entire state of Michigan, because everything within “x” number of miles from an international water way (including the Great Lakes) is fair game.

I tried to find a map of areas where Border Patrol has authority. Most look like this But, it shows half of Wisconsin included, however Lake Michigan is not international waters. And does that restriction include ICE? This would be good stuff for some smarty pants attorney to figure out and share!

This is making the exact error I'm referring to. As defined by the Treaty of Paris, the Webster Ashburton Treaty, the 1908 border treaty, the 1909 boundary waters treaty, and the INA, Lake Michigan is an internal water of the US, just one open to Canadian navigation. It is not international waters.
these old maps are CBP propaganda because they include Chicago as if Lake Michigan were subject to the International Law of the Sea
Pretty much if you're near a large river, or any of the Great lakes, guess what... I also thin any international airport also applies, sona 100 I radius around those too. It's ridiculous.
too bad CPB thinks the great lakes are an international border. Maybe they will think the Mississippi is one to

They have also claimed authority (at times) within 100 miles of an "international port" which includes Airport with international flights, or a port capable of handling international shipping (the Great Lakes, Columbia River and just about the entire Mississippi/Ohio River system)

If that were how it worked then Denver would be in the border zone; everybody agrees it isn't. Chicago (and a lot of the Midwest) is mischaracterized not because of airports but because people misinterpret the 1909 border treaty with Canada. Lake Michigan is an internal water, not international.

Ya it's always blown my mind that anything bordering lake michigan is an "international border" ...Which leaves Michigan pretty fucked as related to border patrol jurisdiction www.michiganpublic.org/news/2018-04...
Trump admin now claims Lake Michigan is international waters and therefore can be monitored by border patrol
I understand they also include international airports. I'm always confused by this map, because is lake Michigan considered international water?

& .mn.gov
has #US under a peacetime “emergency” All the slave catchers have THE MOST POWERS POSSIBLE. We have an international border along Lake Superior the Boundary Waters .bsky.social & Indigenous territory along Canada &

I’m not really certain that CBP’s definition of external boundary excludes international airport, especially since it includes the land border of the completely US territorial side of the Great Lakes, for instance.
Unfortunately they do in fact border two Great Lakes and have Milwaukee, Green Bay and Superior as port cities respectively
They have jurisdiction on any navigable waters in the US. There is a USCG base in Milwaukee on Lake Michigan.

This map is not accurate. Lake Michigan is *not* considered international waters. I was corrected on this by immigration attorneys on here - this is a BIT more complicated! Here is the more accurate map. en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Border_...

“These “external boundaries” include international land borders but also the entire U.S. coastline.”
Lake Michigan. www.aclu.org/know-your-ri...
From Mediterranean shores to Lake Michigan: an international student's story www.wuwm.com/immigration-...

I suspect we're addressing different things. ALL Great Lakes, including Lake Michigan, fall under the jurisdiction of Border Patrol because they're considered international waterways, despite that Lake Michigan does not share any shoreline with Canada.
That Great Lakes region border is slippery. All the waterways.
the great lakes count as an international border also so chicago and milwaukee count

Certain states have intrastate and international boundaries (harder to conceal) Mississippi River, IL River, Great Lakes, Co River, Rio Grande, etc.
Residents in other areas sink wells and counties are not checking. TX is lawless.

So, I was reminded that CBP doesn’t treat all airports like an international borders the way it does the Great Lakes to extend that “reasonable distance” further inland. For when they do work at airports, I stand by it but had to make sure I didn’t give copaganda credit in my own impression.
The fact that they consider all of the Great Lakes the border is wild.
Entirety of Michigan thanks to the Great Lakes counting as an international border :(
Do I understand correctly that these idiots are counting the SHORE of the Great Lakes as a border? As though they have any respect for the law.

Border Patrol does, but only within 100 miles of the international border, Minneapolis is well outside their 100 mile jurisdiction. Even if you accept their idea that the shoreline of the Great Lakes are the international border (which would be saying that the lakes are Canadas)

They consider ocean shorelines including bays - okay “international ports”. And shorelines of each of the Great Lakes, including Lake Michigan - a stretch to include Chicago. But I have heard, that they also consider all “international airports” - Kansas City & Denver are border cities?? Really??
They are trying to claim distance to the great lakes which include an international border, it's all BS. It's how they rationalized the Chicago actions.

The entirety of it is within CBP jurisdiction basically. Most of its boundaries count as an international border. Lots of international ships go on the great lakes and rivers connecting them. Bunch of very busy bridges connecting to Canada. So, yeah, but our senator still sucks.

Right. Coast Guard has jurisdiction in the Great Lakes because they're an INTERNATIONAL BOUNDARY WATER. Also there's an INTERNATIONAL migratory bird refuge along the coast of Erie and Huron. How that's not an international border is a mystery to me
Lake Michigan is entirely within US borders (the only Great Lake that is so). There is not an international border in Milwaukee.
They know that Lake Michigan is NOT international waters, right? 👀
NO, Chicago is NOT within 100 miles of the border. It is within that distance of Lake Michigan but that lake does NOT touch Canada or any other international border.

Gov. Pritzker discussed this in his interview with Tim Miller on the Bulwark podcast. In Chicago, they have redefined Lake Michigan as part of the international border with Canada, so they can operate within 100 miles of the shores of L. Michigan.

.illinois.gov The border enforcement zone is 100 mi from the international borders. Why are the maps calling the shore of Lake Michigan an international border? Lake Michigan is entirely within the US. Logically, nowhere in Illinois should be considered part of the border region.
ALL of the Border Patrol placements are a con job. Illinois is a "border state" because it borders Lake Michigan, which does not include an international border. HMMM??
I still don’t understand how Lake Michigan is an “international border”

I recall how the Border Patrol has long been pleased to claim that the shore of Lake Michigan is somehow equivalent to an international border; to give an excuse for their operating in Chicago. In direct opposition to geography.
They also got the border wrong… Lake Michigan is not international water… 🧐
Starts on the Atlantic. Lake Michigan is totally in the US, yet the entire shore shows as international border. So that`s why I was curious about rivers.
seems to be some shallow waters right on the border - see you there.

Well, devils advocate there. The lake is accessible easily without border control from Canada side Lake Huron through the 4 miles wide exit of Michigan Lake.
So technically shores are laying under the definition of an international border for control...
Yeah twisted, but completly out of touch...

if you read this far we're also in the Great Lakes protective freshwater basin, share an international land border that's hard to monitor, and you're within a day's drive of every major northeastern city. ok that's my elevator pitch
.bsky.social fun fact: the Great Lakes are international waters. That makes the whole state of Michigan within the 100 mile boundary in which customs border patrol can stop you. 1/2
Think about how "the border" is literally everywhere. Oceans, Great Lakes, international airports
The border can mean any port or airport that receives international traffic. Small town airport near Canada. Great Lakes port. CBP doesn't need a warrant or probable cause to seize.
The Great Lakes get you to an international border (Canada)
I understand the confusion. They consider The Great Lakes as an international border. Chicago is within the 100 miles of a navigable water that borders on another country.
Aren’t all the Great Lakes under federal jurisdiction to the international border with Canada?
the "border" includes navigable international waterways, i.e., the Great Lakes.
The Border Patrol pretends that everywhere within 100? 200? miles of an international border is within their jurisdiction and they also pretend the Great Lakes are all international borders

I saw this www.justice.gov/epstein/file... and don't know if it's legit, but it made me wonder why lake Michigan? Are any parts of the great lakes international waters? And is there an age of consent in international waters?
And that “border” includes the Great Lakes and airports that do international flights, so folks who think they’re safe because they don’t live near Canada or Mexico should reconsider.

I hate to say it, but CBP is authorized to operate within 100 miles of any international border. That includes all our coastlines, and the Great Lakes that form part of our border with Canada. They're NOT, however, allowed to execute people exercising their constitutional rights.
Not to mention the fact that they are treating the shooters of Lake Michigan as an international border. It's the only ones if the Great Lakes that is totally within the US.
They treat the fucking great lakes as an international border so none of Michigan is actually free
And DHS is claiming the border on the Great Lakes starts at the shore and not the international line mid-lakes
international airpors are the border. so are the great lakes except michigan. oy
also the thing about international airports isn’t true. the regulation is about external boundaries, which include sea coasts and the great lakes, but not international airports
Drawn as if Lake Michigan has an international border extending to Indiana???
this map makes no sense even on it's own terms, there is no international boundary in lake michigan
With the Strait of Mackinac being way less than 12 miles (probably 1-2?), I never understood why Lake Michigan counts as international.

Chump's stormtroopers are so smart! They found a portal in the middle of Lake Michigan that will transport anyone from any border! Why else would 🧊🛥️ be lurking about THE ONLY GREAT LAKE WITHOUT AN INTERNATIONAL BOUNDARY? 😆🤦🏼‍♀️

Can anyone explain why the red border line on this image (which is everywhere online) goes around Lake Michigan? Lake Michigan is wholly in the US. Chicago, for instance, isn’t within 100 miles of an international border.
Where the fuck is an international border 100 miles from Chicago? Lake Michigan only borders US states.

#CBP: Lake Michigan is the only Great Lake with no international border. It borders on Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, and Wisconsin.
Can #CBP also operate within some distance of an international airport?

"international land borders *and the U.S. coastline*" is the important bit there. You can pilot a ship from Lake Michigan to Canada without touching ground, therefore CPB claims it under their jurisdiction.

The closest international border from Chicago is over 450 miles away. The US Border Control can’t just make up the rules and say the border with Canada is Lake Michigan… 🤦🏻‍♂️ WTAF. They are allowed to operate within 100 miles of an international border. Sault Ste Marie, Ontario. 7-8 hour drive.
Uh if CBP is saying the border is the shores of Lake Michigan does that mean they're ceding the lake to us? Cuz that's not where the international border says it is.

All of Michigan is an international border. I live 180 miles from Detroit, and 180 miles from Sarnia, MI (Port Huron). I live closer to Lake Michigan 82 miles. That's an international border too. Have that make sense. Someone went crazy with a sharpie during Trump 1.
Lake Michigan is not in international waters! All is within domestic borders. 😎
Well Lake Michigan is 100% within the US. So Chicago has no international borders on lake. ￼

www.cbsnews.com/news/federal... LIKE CHICAGO, WHY ARE BORDER PATROL AGENTS ALLOWED TO OPERATE WELL OUTSIDE OF THE 100 MILE OF INTERNATIONAL BORDER LIMIT?!! BOTH CHARLOTTE AND CHICAGO ARE HUNDREDS OF MILES FROM A INTERNATIONAL BORDER. LAKE MICHIGAN BORDER W/CHICAGO IS 100% WITHIN THE U. S. !!!🇺🇸
They consider Lake Michigan to be a border
The southern Lake Michigan shore as "the border" is bullshit. It should be the actual international border, which is bad enough.
forming an international micronation in the middle of Lake Michigan and getting upset at imperialism or something when people tell me nothing there is international water
As I understand it, they count the entire shore of Lake Michigan as part of the border. Makes no sense, but neither does much with this administration.
The closest point on an international border from Minneapolis is a point in the middle of Lake Superior 250 miles away. So that fascist bastard is wrong in more than one way.
